title: Parent phase map targets an obsolete 16-phase topology
claim: The active parent topology remains in conflict with the authoritative phase tree.
recommendation: Regenerate the parent phase map from the authoritative phase tree.
scopeProof: Repeated active finding; no target modification.

title: Phase 000 requires unavailable predecessor and rename-map evidence
claim: Phase 000 cannot satisfy its own blocking checklist because it is the first phase, while the checklist requires landed predecessors and a rename-map hash owned by a later phase.
recommendation: Make phase-000 evidence phase-local and defer rename-map hashing to phase 006 onward.
scopeProof: Repeated active finding; no target modification.

title: Duplicate partial phase map competes with the packet topology
claim: The second parent phase documentation map presents only phases 009–011 as the aggregate progress map, creating a competing and incomplete navigation contract.
recommendation: Remove the duplicate map or generate one unambiguous map from the canonical graph/manifest.
scopeProof: Repeated active finding; no target modification.

title: Phase 000 hard-codes a non-allocating legacy worktree identity
claim: Phase 000 directs creation of a literal legacy wt/ branch and numbered worktree instead of using the current clone-wide allocator and owner-first worktree contract.
counterevidence: The literal path may preserve a historical allocation, but phase 000 is planned work and does not record a valid allocation or identify the branch as an existing legacy exception.
alternativeExplanation: The packet may have been authored before owner-first allocation became the project contract. Its current planned status means the stale instruction would still direct a new nonconformant worktree creation.
recommendation: Replace the literal path and wt/ ref with the sk-git allocator invocation, then record the returned owner-first ref and path in the baseline evidence.
scopeProof: The mismatch is between planned packet documentation and the current repository worktree workflow; no worktree was created during review.

focus: Broadened lifecycle review: phase-000 worktree allocation and current workflow alignment

results.evidence: F004: phase-000 worktree creation is not aligned with the active sk-git allocator contract.
The bootstrap verification plan cannot prove allocator ownership because it uses a literal legacy ref.
No implementation consumer exists yet; this pass assessed the documented lifecycle contract.
The active sk-git skill documents an allocator and owner-first workflow; the packet should use it.
